title: symphoricarpos puniceus
intro: Symphoricarpos puniceus, also known as the wolfberry or red-berried elder, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its clusters of small, red berries and pink or white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The wolfberry has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried berries and capsules, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
